warnings.message:
URDF compile warning (non-blocking): geometry overlap check reported overlaps.
Geometry overlap check failed: part AABBs intersect at sampled articulation poses. worst_pair=('chassis'[10], 'fl_wheel'[0]) min_overlap_depth=0.06639m overlap_volume=0.001992m^3 pose_index=19 pose=(chassis_to_front_l_hub=-0.5, chassis_to_front_r_hub=-0.5, chassis_to_rl_wheel=3.142, chassis_to_rr_wheel=0, chassis_to_steering=-0.6, front_l_hub_to_fl_wheel=3.142, front_r_hub_to_fr_wheel=1.571). elem_a=(name='chassis__visual_11' origin_xyz=(0.7, 0.0, 0.35) origin_rpy=(0.0, 0.0, 0.0) geom=Box) elem_b=(name='fl_wheel__visual_1' origin_xyz=(0.0, 0.0, 0.0) origin_rpy=(1.5708, 0.0, 0.0) geom=Cylinder). hint: try moving 'fl_wheel' by ~0.07139m along world y (or adjust the responsible articulation origin/limits / collision origins). Note: this QC is conservative (bounding boxes); concave mesh collisions can yield false positives. Fix by adjusting articulation origins/limits and/or visual/collision origins so parts do not interpenetrate in the rest pose or across the articulated range.
If this overlap is acceptable (e.g., conservative false-positive), explicitly allow the specific pair in `run_tests()` via `ctx.allow_overlap(link_a, link_b, reason=...)` and then run `ctx.check_no_overlaps(...)` so the allowance is tracked; otherwise adjust geometry/joints.
